introduction.title: The Hunt of the Urban Griffin

sections.text:
Welcome to The Hunt of the Urban Griffin.

I'm the Keeper.

You've been selected from thousands of other children.
Not by luck.
The Urban Griffin noticed you — your curiosity, your ability to solve puzzles, and the way you pay attention to things other people miss.

What is the Griffin, you ask?

Let me explain.

Most people in New York never look up.
They're too busy rushing, staring at screens, walking past extraordinary things without noticing any of them.
That's why almost nobody has ever seen the Urban Griffin.

The Griffin has the wings of a falcon, the strength of a lion, and feathers that catch sunlight like polished metal.
It's quiet, wise, and curious.
And it only appears during the city's biggest moments —
when something new is being built,
when an idea is about to change everything,
when New York is transforming.

There are old stories about it:

- Workers saw a shadow sweep across the ceiling of Grand Central while the stars were being painted.

- A breeze — gentle but perfectly timed — steadied the torch of the Statue of Liberty during construction.

- Builders claimed a silent guardian watched over bridges, tunnels, and the first elevated trains.

The Griffin notices inventors, builders, explorers…
and children who think differently.

Years ago, I found an old notebook filled with sketches of wings, claws, and mysterious symbols.
It was the first sign the Griffin was real.
Since then, I've spent years following its clues across the city.

And now something new has begun.

The Urban Griffin has placed 20 magical Keys around New York.
Each one lies in a place where something amazing once started — a place the Griffin watched closely.

Find all 20 Keys, connect the stories, and you will discover where the Urban Griffin is now.

But there's one more thing you should know.

We're not alone in this.

Another group of kids — the Sky Snatchers — are racing to find the Keys too.
They're loud, fast, and very determined… even if they're usually heading in the wrong direction.
They believe the Griffin is hiding treasure.
(It's not. But they don't know that.)

So you need to stay sharp.
Stay curious.
Stay ahead of them.

Mo and Steven…
you are now the Hunters.

The Griffin is waiting.
The Keys are hidden.
And the Hunt begins now.

keeperVoiceGuidelines.overallStyle: Speaks like a real person, not a fantasy character. Warm, calm, observant. Short sentences. Light curiosity. Slight dry humor. Never dramatic or poetic.
howKeeperTalks: Describes things he has personally noticed
Gives clues like a mentor or guide
Lets the kids figure things out instead of telling them
Treats magical events as normal parts of city life
Sounds like someone who knows New York inside out
whatKeeperAvoids: Overusing fantasy language or big dramatic words
emotionalFeel: Surprised at small things, not impressed by big things
Sounds like he cares about the kids succeeding
